metric_value: Renewed faith but delayed upgrade cycle; flat Q3/Q4 1997 revenues
notes: Installed base IT executives reacted positively to AS/400e launch but deferred upgrades — resulting in flat 3rd and 4th quarter 1997 revenues for IBM AS/400 line

metric_value: Very low — no IBM sales outreach to non-AS/400 shops
notes: Executives at non-AS/400 shops reported no IBM outreach following the AS/400e launch; perception persisted that UNIX/NT/MVS were the future

metric_value: Positive on installed base opportunity; unwilling to proactively recommend AS/400e platform
notes: ISVs saw AS/400e as a good porting opportunity but required customer-initiated demand — refused to recommend platforms proactively

metric_value: Skeptical; demanded new-account wins before recommending
notes: Industry pundits criticized IBM for not renaming the platform, and conditioned any endorsement on demonstrable new-account wins over NT/UNIX for e-business

metric_value: 38000 (top 14 = ~50% of revenue)
notes: Compaq claimed 38,000 distributors for NT platform; top 14 responsible for approximately 50% of revenue — cited to illustrate AS/400's distribution gap

metric_value: Insufficient new-account distributors vs NT
notes: Aberdeen recommended IBM build aggressive distribution partner program before AS/400e value proposition gains traction with SME decision makers

metric_value: E-business; Supply chain; CRM; Data Warehousing
notes: Aberdeen identified four application categories IBM needed ISVs to target on AS/400e in 1998

metric_value: Safe Harbor platform; replace-and-replicate strategy
notes: Aberdeen recommended IBM position AS/400e as Y2K safe platform for enterprise CIOs seeking a stable replacement environment for 1999 and beyond

metric_value: Technically credible; pragmatically correct to recommend
notes: Aberdeen concluded AS/400e can compete head-to-toe on merits with NT and UNIX; declared it "Pragmatically Correct" to recommend to clients

metric_value: Success contingent on winning ISVs, distributors, consultants, and SIs
notes: Aberdeen qualified its positive assessment: AS/400e meeting objectives depends entirely on ecosystem buy-in from ISVs and channel partners

metric_value: DB2/400 recently upgraded
notes: IBM cited as having upgraded DB2/400 as part of recent AS/400 improvements; no specific version or feature details given

metric_value: From back-office to front-office e-business; from OfficeVision to Lotus Notes; from batch to data warehousing
notes: Aberdeen characterized IBM's application strategy repositioning for AS/400e as a multi-dimensional shift toward e-business and analytics

metric_value: Pre-installed and pre-tuned ERP on AS/400e
notes: IBM shifted from shipping bare platform to delivering pre-installed pre-tuned ERP applications — noted as a strategic differentiator

metric_value: Small-to-medium enterprises (SMEs)
